proguard.evaluation.executor

Class Executor

  • java.lang.Object
    • proguard.evaluation.executor.Executor
  • Direct Known Subclasses:
    ReflectionExecutor

    public abstract class Executor
extends java.lang.Object
    This abstract class specifies a modular component which can be added to a ExecutingInvocationUnit in order to extend its capabilities. An Executor specifies which method calls it supports, what methods return their own instance and how a method result is calculated.

    • Nested Class Summary

      Nested Classes 
      Modifier and Type Class and Description
      static class  Executor.Builder<T extends Executor>
      A builder for the executor.

    • Method Detail

      • getMethodResult

        public abstract java.util.Optional<Value> getMethodResult(MethodExecutionInfo methodData,
                                                          ReferenceValue instance,
                                                          AnalyzedObject callingInstance,
                                                          Value[] parameters,
                                                          java.util.function.Function<java.lang.Object,Value> valueCalculator)
        Calculate the result of a given method. This is the return value for a non-constructor method (null if it returns void) and the instantiated object for a constructor.
        Parameters:
        methodData - Information about the called method.
        instance - The ReferenceValue of the instance, null for static methods.
        callingInstance - The instance object, null for static methods.
        parameters - An array of the parameter values of the method call.
        valueCalculator - a function mapping the result of a method invocation (can be an Object with the result if the executor calculates a real value or a Model) to the appropriate Value used by the analysis.
        Returns:
        The result of the method call wrapped in an optional. Optional.empty() if a result could not be calculated.

      • isSupportedMethodCall

        public boolean isSupportedMethodCall(MethodSignature signature)
        Returns whether a certain method invocation is supported.
        Parameters:
        signature - The method signature.
        Returns:
        whether the method invocation is supported.

      • returnsOwnInstance

        public boolean returnsOwnInstance(MethodSignature signature,
                                  boolean returnsSameTypeAsInstance)
        Returns whether a certain method invocation is assumed to return its own instance. This indicates whether replacing the instance reference in the stack and variables is necessary.
        Parameters:
        signature - The method signature.
        returnsSameTypeAsInstance - whether the method has matching return and instance types
        Returns:
        whether the method returns its own instance.

      • getInstanceCopyIfMutable

        public abstract java.util.Optional<AnalyzedObject> getInstanceCopyIfMutable(ReferenceValue instanceValue)
        Get an object which will act as the calling instance. If we know that executing the method does not modify the instance this can just be the same value. Otherwise, a copy should be returned in order to not change the reference whose state may be of interest at the end of an analysis.
        Parameters:
        instanceValue - The ReferenceValue of the instance.
        Returns:
        The new calling instance.
